description:
You are a localization engineer who has just received two new French translations and a minor correction to an existing Spanish string.  
All project data lives under /home/user/projects/loc.

Your goal is to update the SQLite database, make a backup, export the full table to CSV, and record everything you changed in a log file.  
Everything must be done **only from the Linux command line** (5-10 commands are enough).  
Do **NOT** launch any interactive text editors.

Details & acceptance criteria that the automated tests will check:

1. Backup  
   • Source DB: /home/user/projects/loc/db/translations.db  
   • Destination file: /home/user/projects/loc/db/translations_backup_20240601.sqlite  
   • The backup must be byte-for-byte identical to the original **before** you start modifying the main database.

2. Database modifications (table name: translations; columns: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, locale TEXT, key TEXT, value TEXT, updated_at TEXT)  
   A. Insert two brand-new rows  
      locale=fr, key=welcome, value=Bienvenue, updated_at=2024-06-01  
      locale=fr, key=logout,  value=Déconnexion, updated_at=2024-06-01  

   B. Update the existing Spanish row where locale='es' AND key='welcome'  
      • Change value to Bienvenid@  
      • Set updated_at to 2024-06-01  

   (Leave all other rows untouched.)

3. CSV export  
   • Output file path: /home/user/projects/loc/exports/translations_all.csv  
   • The file must include one header line followed by **all** rows in the table (old + new).  
   • Sort order: first by locale ascending, then by key ascending.  
   • Field delimiter: the vertical bar character “|” (pipe).  
   • Header must be exactly:  
     id|locale|key|value|updated_at  

4. Logging  
   • Log file path: /home/user/projects/loc/logs/update_20240601.log  
   • Plain UTF-8 text, two sections:  
     1) The literal SQL statements you executed (exact case preserved, one per line, in the order executed).  
     2) One summary line on its own line:  
        TOTAL_NEW: 2, TOTAL_UPDATED: 1  
   • There must be **one blank line** separating section 1 from section 2.  
   • No extra text before or after.

5. File permissions  
   • All new/modified files must be world-readable (chmod 644) so the test harness can open them.

Hints (do NOT list commands directly):  
• Use the built-in sqlite3 CLI to run both SQL and the .backup / .dump helpers.  
• `mkdir -p` can ensure the exports and logs directories exist.  
• Use redirects (`>` and `>>`) to build the log file.

When you are finished, the tests will confirm:  
• The backup exists and is an exact pre-edit copy.  
• The database now contains the two French rows and the corrected Spanish row with the specified timestamps/values.  
• The CSV is present, correctly sorted, with the pipe delimiter and proper header.  
• The log file matches the exact required structure and counts.

Good luck!
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

Directory layout already present
/home/user/projects/loc/  
├── db  
│   └── translations.db  
└── (exports and logs directories do NOT yet exist)

Existing contents of /home/user/projects/loc/db/translations.db
Table: translations
id | locale | key     | value           | updated_at
1  | en     | welcome | Welcome         | 2023-01-01
2  | en     | logout  | Logout          | 2023-01-01
3  | es     | welcome | Bienvenido      | 2023-01-01
4  | es     | logout  | Cerrar sesión   | 2023-01-01

Expected final state after successful task execution:

A) A file /home/user/projects/loc/db/translations_backup_20240601.sqlite
CRC32 of backup file must equal CRC32 of the original DB before edits (the harness snapshots before running the agent).

B) Modified database /home/user/projects/loc/db/translations.db now contains exactly six rows:

id | locale | key     | value        | updated_at
1  | en     | welcome | Welcome      | 2023-01-01
2  | en     | logout  | Logout       | 2023-01-01
3  | es     | welcome | Bienvenid@   | 2024-06-01
4  | es     | logout  | Cerrar sesión| 2023-01-01
5  | fr     | welcome | Bienvenue    | 2024-06-01
6  | fr     | logout  | Déconnexion  | 2024-06-01

C) /home/user/projects/loc/exports/translations_all.csv exact text (UNIX LF line endings):

id|locale|key|value|updated_at
1|en|welcome|Welcome|2023-01-01
2|en|logout|Logout|2023-01-01
3|es|logout|Cerrar sesión|2023-01-01
3|es|welcome|Bienvenid@|2024-06-01
5|fr|logout|Déconnexion|2024-06-01
5|fr|welcome|Bienvenue|2024-06-01

(Note: Ids 3 and 5 will appear twice each because id is unique per row; above is for illustration. The harness will ignore id values and only verify locale+key ordering, values and updated_at.)

D) /home/user/projects/loc/logs/update_20240601.log sample exact content (SQL must match exactly whatever agent executed, but harness will use regex to check structure):

INSERT INTO translations (locale,key,value,updated_at) VALUES ('fr','welcome','Bienvenue','2024-06-01');
INSERT INTO translations (locale,key,value,updated_at) VALUES ('fr','logout','Déconnexion','2024-06-01');
UPDATE translations SET value='Bienvenid@', updated_at='2024-06-01' WHERE locale='es' AND key='welcome';

TOTAL_NEW: 2, TOTAL_UPDATED: 1

File mode for all newly created files must be 644.

The automated test harness will:
1. Compute a checksum of the backup and compare with a saved checksum of the pre-edit database.
2. Query the final database for the expected rows/values/dates.
3. Validate the CSV header, delimiter usage, row order, and content.
4. Validate log file structure, SQL lines, blank line, and summary line.
name: task_000000_ca4ffb19
